dialogos.texto_en: The Oceanic Culture Museum was a fascinating facility.
I learned a lot from the exhibits\non the history and culture of the maritime people.
I had no idea\nthat there were so many different types of canoes.
It's amazing how much the shape of something\ncan change depending on the nation\nthat made it, and where it was made.
I also found the planetarium\nto be very interesting and enjoyable.
Yeah, especially seeing the starry sky of Okinawa\nfor the first time.
You can't see it well from the hotel,\nwith all the city lights.
Also%p-2;――
The combination of the history and culture of the people\nwho traveled across the vast ocean with the beautiful\nstarry sky in the planetarium was perfect.
The sea and the stars, huh?\nWhat makes you think of that combo?
...No way, you really don't remember?
Huh?
Well, people used stars as navigation markers\nwhen traveling on the sea, right? I learned that\nfrom you when we went to the island last summer.
Oh, I remember that now.
Stars are indispensable for sea travel.\nSo, it makes sense to have a planetarium here.
Y-yeah, you're right.
Uesugi-kun?\nWhy is your face red?
Ah, it's just that I'm happy\nthat you remembered what I taught you, Itsuki.
Of course I remember.\nIt was a fascinating story.
I-I see... Is that so?
(It's so heartwarming to know...\n that someone remembers\n a casual conversation like that.)
It seems you haven't forgotten, then.
Well, I didn't forget it completely.
Haha, just kidding.
While thinking about the sea and stars\nin the planetarium, I had a thought%p-2;――
I suppose that's how some constellations were created \nwhen people sailed the seas.
Constellations?
When I think of constellations,\nonly the famous ones come to mind%p-2;――
In reality, it's up to each individual\nto connect the stars and create constellations, right?
So I was thinking, maybe there were as many\nconstellations as there were people who looked up\nat the sky while traveling on the sea.
Ahh, I see.
Maybe they drew their own dreams\non the canvas of the starry sky\nwhile alone on their ships.
I wonder.\nMaybe there wouldn't have been such a luxury.
Uesugi-kun, you sure are practical, aren't you.
But, certainly, even in the museum,\nthey introduced the harshness of old sea voyages.
...Well. If we're gonna exchange our opinions here,\ntime'll pass by quickly.
Yes, let's move on to the next thing.
A constellation just for them.\nThen, couldn't they draw it in the current sky?
Hehe, that's right. If we remove the preconception\nthat there are predetermined constellations,\nwe might be able to find them.
Maybe, the same constellations that the people\nfrom the past saw might come to mind.
There's no way to confirm that,\nbut it would be really wonderful if it happened.
...Well. If we're gonna exchange our opinions here,\ntime'll pass by quickly.
Yes, let's move on to the next thing.
You have a surprisingly rich imagination.\nI never thought of that.
Is it really that surprising?\nI also think about a lot of things.
No, it's not like I wanted to say it was weird,\nI just...thought it was charming.
Huh? Charming?
There's still a lot about you, Itsuki,\nthat I don't know yet.
Thinking about being able to get to know\nthat Itsuki from now on\nmakes me happy.
...M-me too.\nI also want to get to know the you\nthat I don't know yet.
I-is that so.
...Well. If we're gonna exchange our opinions here,\ntime'll pass by quickly.
Yes, let's move on to the next thing.
